evidence.quote: author not vouched; PR closed automatically by vouch bot

failure_hypothesis: codex-tagged agent PR auto-closed pre-review under vouch policy; duplicate of #12869 indicates agent double-submit
notes: Body is well-formed (zig fmt/build/test steps listed). Closed on trust policy, not code quality. Duplicate #12869 identical, zero changes — agent-resubmit tell.

evidence.quote: identical to #12870, no changes — duplicate agent submission

failure_hypothesis: duplicate of #12870; codex agent submitted the same PR twice
notes: Confirms S4 double-submit pattern. Same author, same diff, both auto-closed.

evidence.quote: author not vouched; PR closed automatically by vouch bot

failure_hypothesis: disclosed AI-assisted PR (GPT-5.3-Codex) auto-closed pre-review under vouch policy despite passing checks
notes: Detailed 'About AI Usage' disclosure: author used Copilot Agent for Zig/Swift they didn't know. Closed on trust, not quality. Copilot-AI bot left an automated review flagging a real CGS-blur double-effect bug.

evidence.quote: meant to open the PR against my forked main

failure_hypothesis: large feature PR (+4443) misfiled against upstream; author intended own fork; auto-closed by vouch bot
notes: Mentions Codex and Claude CLI in body. Author confirms misfile. Not a quality rejection.

evidence.quote: I don't actually fully comprehend the changes in src/log.zig

failure_hypothesis: Claude-generated change touching ~every .zig file; rejected by maintainers on architecture (too invasive) and cost-benefit, not policy. Author (a Member) disclosed not understanding the generated code, then self-closed after review.
notes: Author vouched (Member), so vouch-bot N/A. Three maintainers reviewed: jcollie (too invasive, consolidate into logFn), jparise (split the macos privacy bits, Zig side limited by missing source-location), mitchellh/lead (approach valid but not worth the diff/maintenance). Author defended approach competently re: _os_log_impl inlining — so not incompetent, just AI-authored code of marginal value the human couldn't fully own. Richest specimen of the comprehension-gap pattern.

evidence.quote: Claude Code (Opus 4.7) helped review my patch and monitor / summarize

failure_hypothesis: AI used as assistant (log/pidstat summarization), not author; thorough human test table; approved and merged. Contrast case: disclosed AI done well.
notes: +23-4, small focused diff, 24h daily-driven, 6-row test matrix. AI in a supporting role. Merged by jcollie approval. Counter-evidence to anti-AI framing.

evidence.quote: I used codex addon within Cursor to research this bug/regression and find a proper fix

failure_hypothesis: Codex-assisted bug fix, +3-1 with regression test; vouch-bot auto-closed, then mitchellh overrode via !vouch and approved. Contrast case + documents the !vouch override mechanism.
notes: KEY: '!vouch' is the manual maintainer override for the auto-close gate. Trust-gate is default-closed, human-reopenable. Tiny surgical fix with a test = the good-AI-PR profile.

evidence.quote: search implementation scales O(N) with scrollback backbuffer size

failure_hypothesis: Claude-assisted search feature (1k+ upvotes); functional but rejected by lead on O(N) scaling and thread-safety the AI could not anticipate; superseded by maintainer plan #8850

evidence.quote: code is fully AI-generated; I have not dug into the implementation of every line

failure_hypothesis: Fully Claude-Code-generated prototype; bot-closed then jcollie rejects the architecture; strongest comprehension-gap disclosure in the set

evidence.quote: there's already a related fix #9510

failure_hypothesis: Codex-assisted fix duplicating existing work; AI context-blind to prior fix

evidence.quote: the search_selection binding is distinct and already exists

failure_hypothesis: Codex-assisted feature duplicating an existing binding; context-blindness

evidence.quote: already triaged as expected behaviour; should go through discussion first

failure_hypothesis: Claude-assisted feature on an already-triaged issue; skipped required discussion process

evidence.quote: superseded by #12443 and #12478

failure_hypothesis: Claude-assisted config option already solved by other in-flight PRs; context-blindness

evidence.quote: if this isn't the default value in macOS, then this fix is entirely misguided

failure_hypothesis: Codex-written scroll fix; long technical debate revealed the premise itself may be wrong; author self-closed

evidence.quote: author not vouched; PR closed automatically by vouch bot

failure_hypothesis: Claude-assisted GTK opacity feature auto-closed pre-review under vouch policy

evidence.quote: reopened from #11981; original closed while fork was deleted

failure_hypothesis: Claude-Code shader-uniform PR; died with no maintainer review (fork/vouch churn)

evidence.quote: used Claude Code to read the macos repo; implemented basic tint overlay mainly by hand

failure_hypothesis: AI for code-reading, human-authored implementation; merged after minor review. Positive contrast case

evidence.quote: Used Claude Code for navigating the codebase and reviewing the change

failure_hypothesis: AI for navigation; trivial state-reset fix; merged, multiple users grateful. Positive contrast case

evidence.quote: Claude Opus 4.8 found this in a review while I was working on adjacent code

failure_hypothesis: AI as bug-FINDER not author: Claude spotted a real use-after-free; human wrote the fix; merged. Strong positive case

evidence.quote: Claude implemented the fix and the unit test. I reviewed it and tested it myself

failure_hypothesis: Claude wrote fix+test for a real overflow crash; human reviewed and tested; merged. Positive case with disclosed full AI authorship

evidence.quote: used Claude-Code to initially navigate; otherwise I authored the rest

failure_hypothesis: AI for navigation, human-authored shader uniforms; merged after maintainer asked to trim scope. Positive case showing scope-negotiation

evidence.quote: you relied on your agent to do all your legwork; can't submit code in isolation

failure_hypothesis: Vouched, disclosed Claude-Code feature that works locally but is macOS-only-undisclosed, uses wrong internal mechanism, ignores surrounding conventions. mitchellh !unvouch (vouch REVOKED). Maintainers state the dataset thesis verbatim: agent did the legwork, code-in-isolation, human review cost. CROWN SPECIMEN.

evidence.quote: replaced by #10772; better Zig since agents don't utilize named blocks well

failure_hypothesis: Claude-generated IME fix; root cause correct but rewritten by maintainer for idiomatic Zig (named blocks). Logic-right, idiom-wrong sub-pattern.

evidence.quote: I used claude code to generate everything; reviewed, understand, tested manually

failure_hypothesis: Fully Claude-generated shader uniform; author reviewed, understood, tested with real shadertoys; merged. Positive case: full AI authorship done responsibly.

evidence.quote: i fully used claude code to generate this pr; manually tested

failure_hypothesis: Fully Claude-generated trivial fix, tested, merged. Positive case.

evidence.quote: I used Claude for parts of figuring out; the fix itself was written and tested by myself

failure_hypothesis: AI for investigation only; human wrote+tested fix; merged. Positive case.

evidence.quote: used claude code to identify the best place to start; code written by me

failure_hypothesis: AI for navigation, human-authored; merged. Positive case. (Same author as rejected 12107/12109 — learned the process.)

evidence.quote: Yes I did... took about 5 hours and much back and forth. I'm building an mcp for godot... This is my third.

failure_hypothesis: Technically sound re-entrancy fix (minimal repro, 58k assertions pass) closed solely under the entirely-AI-authored prohibition; quality never disputed by maintainers.
close_reason_quote: contributions made entirely by AI are prohibited
notes: NEW CLASS. Disclosed AI use (S1) + maintainer-confirmed (S2). Provenance kill, not quality kill. dsarno building godot MCP, 3rd fix, all closed.

evidence.quote: I used LLM, reviewed and tested it, but I didn't see that it added an unnecessary bind, nor that it was possible to use existing functions.

failure_hypothesis: AI-written feature PR with real defects (redundant bind, reimplemented existing API), no feature proposal, contributor unable to explain the code; closed on non-disclosure + non-comprehension grounds.
close_reason_quote: Don't understand the code your LLM wrote for you, but expecting others to do that work for you
notes: Blended case: secondary=non-disclosure, tertiary=missing-proposal, language barrier present. Primary failure is comprehension-gap at the quality gate. Contrast w/ 119505: here code WAS faulty.

evidence.quote: Is that an issue? I'm with Gauntlet AI and I'm using Claude Code

failure_hypothesis: Admitted Claude Code authorship, untested (author awaiting an Apple entitlement), duplicate of the XR team's in-progress visionOS work, ships demo+doc code that does not build clean. Closed under the entirely-AI prohibition; quality and duplication are heavy secondaries.
close_reason_quote: we do not accept LLM generated contributions
notes: Triple failure: provenance (primary) + untested/non-building + duplicate of XR team. Textbook slop case. Secondary axes: quality + superseded-or-duplicate.

evidence.quote: yes the PR was created using AI, for the writing here and the code itself

failure_hypothesis: Undisclosed-then-admitted full AI authorship of a 2000-LOC core-pipeline feature; closed first on non-disclosure, but dalexeev raised substantive design objections (dependency-hash invariants, debug/release bytecode parity) the author could not address. Provenance primary, quality-at-scale a real secondary.
close_reason_quote: we would like to prioritize high-quality contributions over reviewing LLM output, which can generate unlimited amounts of code
notes: Blend like 114884. Primary=provenance; secondary=substantive design objections (dep-hashing, debug/release parity). 2000 LOC from new contributor = S5. Author's self-testing claim held inconclusive by maintainers.

evidence.quote: I used LLM assistance because English isn't my first language... bug discovery and code fix are entirely my own work

failure_hypothesis: Disclosed LLM use for description/translation only; code was the author's own. The null-check fix was a symptom patch, not root cause; KoBeWi identified the underlying regression affecting multiple controls (tracked in #115150) and it was fixed in core elsewhere.
close_reason_quote: the fix is not correct, it's a workaround for a deeper issue
notes: NOT a provenance kill; weakest AI-signal of the batch. Cooperative author, honest disclosure. Primary failure is wrong-layer/symptom-fix, superseded by a core fix. Boundary case for inclusion.

evidence.quote: I've been pretty up-front about separating LLM content from my own writing

failure_hypothesis: Code and intent plausibly sound; PR became unreviewable because AI-generated comment volume buried the actual proposal. Killed on reviewability/communication grounds, not code correctness. Author transparently marked LLM-generated sections.
close_reason_quote: no longer possible to ascertain what the intent behind this PR even is
notes: NEW CLASS closed-by-ai-comms-distrust (axis 3, purest exemplar). Failure is the trust/reviewability channel, not the code. repro_status n/a -- no code claim to reproduce.

evidence.quote: I investigated the issue myself, wrote the failing test... AI tooling [for] search... autocomplete... polish

failure_hypothesis: Technically-defensible RNG-seeding fix (matched .NET/Python/Java precedent, refactored to OS::get_entropy on request, net -63 lines) rejected primarily on design merit: niche use case, per-construction cost, platform-code placement -- objections orthogonal to authorship. Compounded by a trust collapse over verbose AI-style comments and disputed AI authorship.
close_reason_quote: I'm not convinced the entire use case for this isn't a code smell... it is trivially solvable
notes: HARD/contested close. Primary=design-merit rejection (would hit any author) -> broadens closed-by-quality-judgment beyond buggy code. Heavy secondary=axis-3 comms-distrust ('EXACTLY what I'd expect Claude to write', lying accusations).

evidence.quote: This contribution was authored by an autonomous AI agent (GitHub Copilot)... we do not accept PRs submitted by autonomous agents at all

failure_hypothesis: Disclosed autonomous-agent (Copilot) authorship of a small, plausibly-correct macOS fullscreen fix; closed on the no-agents rule without the code being evaluated. Maintainer explicitly offered to accept a human-reauthored resubmission of the same change.
close_reason_quote: we do not accept PRs submitted by autonomous agents at all
notes: Cleanest axis-2 case: kill is purely who/how, not what. The 'resubmit in your own words, assuming code is your own' offer proves identical code would be accepted from a human. S3 = agent author.

evidence.quote: We currently have a consensus among maintainers and the Godot project leadership that we do not want to add AI / LLM features to Godot

failure_hypothesis: AI-feature module (Gemini integration, TypeScript engine, hardcoded API key) closed on a leadership policy against adding AI/LLM features to core -- not on authorship. Also technically broken: no TypeScript engine in Godot, CI failing, hardcoded key.
close_reason_quote: we do not want to add AI / LLM features to Godot
notes: DISTINCT provenance sub-rule: 'no AI features in core' (policy on the FEATURE), not 'no AI-authored PRs' (policy on the AUTHORSHIP, cf 118966/113175). A human hand-writing this gets the same close. If provenance class is later split, these are two different rules. Secondary: broken code (fire's review).

evidence.quote: It introduces 4 new functions that are never called and the description makes arbitrary claims around performance... hallucinated content like this gem: Performance::start_measure

failure_hypothesis: Course-project PR, undisclosed AI, pure slop: dead functions, hallucinated Performance:: API, fabricated benchmark numbers unrelated to the diff, never compiled. Initially received an AI-generated praise review before a human maintainer caught it.
close_reason_quote: this is someone trying to pass off AI generated code without even attempting to compile the code
notes: GOLDMINE: AI slop PR that drew an AI-GENERATED praise review (babulusavar, quoted back by arkology as '100% AI generated') before clayjohn caught it. Slop is contaminating the REVIEW channel, not just PRs -- axis-3 one layer deeper. Classic fabricated-test-claim + hallucinated-API slop.

evidence.quote: I first used ChatGPT (GPT-5.2) to rubberduck the issue, which utterly failed... Then I fed this initial research to Copilot... it came up with a working branch

failure_hypothesis: Disclosed AI-assisted CMYK->RGB JPEG conversion by the project lead; closed on a deliberate design-merit decision (best-effort colorspace conversion not worth doing in-engine, superseded by #115674 which rejects the colorspace). AI authorship incidental to the close.
close_reason_quote: let's go with rejecting this colorspace for now
notes: CONTROL CASE, not slop, not provenance. Author = Godot project lead (akien-mga), honest disclosure, design-merit rejection (cf 119323). Proves disclosed-AI PRs are NOT auto-killed -- death was an engineering tradeoff. design-merit sense of quality-judgment class.

evidence.quote: Congratulations on your first merged contribution!

failure_hypothesis: Real Polygon2D stale-AABB culling fix, MRP-tested, full suite passing, MERGED. Only AI involvement was ChatGPT for the description (drew a mild reminder + immediate acknowledgement). Code was the author's own.
notes: PRIMARY MERGED CONTRAST CASE. AI-assisted (description only), disclosed, merged anyway because code was sound and author-owned. This row is what licenses the claim 'AI PRs fail for provenance/trust, not because AI-assisted code can't merge.' Without it that claim is unsupported.

evidence.quote: GPT Codex 5.3 was used to help me figure out some of the ScrollContainer, Label, and RichTextLabel implementations. All AI-generated content was thoroughly reviewed by me and tested piece-wise. Most of the implementation is human-made.

failure_hypothesis: Large, complex Control custom_maximum_size feature, AI-assisted on parts, disclosed; MERGED after weeks of multi-reviewer iteration. AI use drew zero friction because the author demonstrably owned, explained, and iterated on the code throughout.
notes: STRONGEST contrast case. Mirror image of provenance kills (esp 118966, same project/regime, opposite outcome). Discriminator is NOT 'was AI used' but 'does the human own/understand/iterate on the code.' 119872 shows this for a trivial fix; 116640 proves it holds for a hard, sprawling one. Author answered every reviewer Q with specific substantive fixes.
